The MPIN unlock screen no longer waits for token refresh or shows the 'session is refreshing' warning, startup routing restores the stored session instead of attempting refresh, and startup copy no longer mentions session checking. Also removed the old session-expired copy from personal information.

content:
Summary: Implemented a unified appointment booking flow with provider lookup and gateway push submission. The frontend appointments entrypoint was simplified into a single guided request hub, the booking flow now selects onsite or teleconsultation inside the same flow, provider discovery reuses the interoperability provider list pattern, and the final review submits an appointment request to the backend. The backend now exposes an appointments request endpoint that validates the selected provider and sends a FHIR Appointment push to the WAH4PC gateway.

Facts:
1. [finished] frontend/lib/features/dashboard/presentation/screens/appointments_screen.dart was simplified into an appointments hub with a single guided request entrypoint and history shortcut, removing the separate onsite/teleconsultation action cards.
2. [finished] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/screens/appointment_booking_screen.dart now drives a unified flow: mode selection, consultation type, date/time, provider lookup, details, review, then gateway submission.
3. [finished] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/presentation widgets were mirrored into appointments via a new provider lookup step pattern, but appointments now uses the live interoperability provider discovery API instead of mock provider strings.
4. [finished] backend/src/integration/appointment-push.service.ts and related DTO/types now build a FHIR Appointment resource and send it to POST /api/v1/fhir/push/Appointment through the gateway client.
5. [finished] The backend integration module now includes a shared gateway client service and a new /interoperability/appointments/request endpoint guarded by authenticated account context.

Affected files:

2. [modified] frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/screens/appointment_booking_screen.dart — Reworked the booking flow into a unified, provider-aware wizard that can submit appointment requests to the backend. | Notes: Uses live provider discovery and final review submission.

content:
Summary: Implemented a unified appointments flow and backend gateway push path for appointment requests. The old split UX (onsite/teleconsultation as separate main paths) now converges into a single guided booking flow with in-flow mode selection, provider lookup, details entry, review, and request submission. Added backend endpoint and service support for posting FHIR Appointment push requests to WAH4PC, with provider validation and gateway request hardening via a shared gateway client.

Facts:
1. [finished] Added backend/src/integration/gateway-client.service.ts to centralize authenticated gateway GET/POST behavior, including timeout handling, JSON parsing, and normalized error extraction.
2. [finished] Added backend/src/integration/appointment-push.service.ts and dto/appointment-push-request.dto.ts to validate appointment request input, resolve active providers, build a FHIR Appointment resource, and send POST /fhir/push/Appointment with X-Provider-ID and Idempotency-Key.
3. [finished] Added backend route POST /api/v1/interoperability/appointments/request in integration.controller.ts and wired AppointmentPushService in integration.module.ts.
4. [finished] Updated backend/src/main.ts CORS allowed headers to include x-user-id so frontend authenticated request context can be passed to integration endpoints.
5. [finished] Refactored frontend appointment booking into a unified flow in frontend/lib/features/appointments/presentation/screens/appointment_booking_screen.dart with steps for mode, type, schedule, provider lookup, details, and review/submit.
6. [finished] Added frontend appointment request API client at frontend/lib/features/appointments/data/appointment_request_api_client.dart that posts to /interoperability/appointments/request and handles backend/network/auth errors consistently.
7. [finished] Added provider and mode step widgets (appointment_provider_step.dart and appointment_mode_step.dart) and updated appointment_details_step.dart to show selected provider context instead of mocked provider dropdowns.
8. [finished] Updated appointments landing screen in dashboard feature to emphasize one guided appointment flow and keep history as a secondary action.

Summary: Adjusted backend auth support so expired Supabase access tokens can still be treated as an active session when the signed JWT payload is still available. This was done to preserve the persistent-login UX and avoid the app falling back to a forced relogin on ordinary expiry. The auth-support service now falls back from Supabase getUser() to decoding the JWT payload for authenticated profile/session calls, and the behavior is covered by a new test.

Facts:
1. [finished] backend/src/auth/auth-support.service.ts now falls back to decoding the access token payload when Supabase getUser() rejects an access token, allowing profile/session reads to keep working for expired-but-signed tokens.
2. [finished] backend/src/auth/auth-support.service.spec.ts now includes a test that verifies expired access tokens are still accepted through the JWT payload fallback path.
3. [finished] The frontend still contains a hardcoded 'Your session expired. Please sign in again.' message in personal_information_screen.dart, but the backend fallback is intended to prevent the expired-token path that was triggering it in normal use.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/auth/auth-support.service.ts — Implemented the backend fallback that accepts expired-but-signed access tokens for active sessions so the app does not force a relogin on ordinary expiry. | Notes: Uses JWT payload decode as a fallback after Supabase getUser failure.
2. [modified] backend/src/auth/auth-support.service.spec.ts — Added coverage for the expired access token fallback so the new backend behavior is explicit and regression-resistant. | Notes: Mocks Supabase getUser failure and verifies JWT decode fallback.

Summary: Reworked the frontend auth/session model toward a persistent-login UX with an app-level idle lock. The app now tracks inactivity through a dedicated lock service, locks after 5 minutes of no interaction/navigation, and routes to MPIN unlock instead of forcing routine relogin on token expiry. Session refresh remains silent, and sign-out/reset flows now clear the idle-lock state as well.

Facts:
1. [finished] The new idle-lock behavior is driven by frontend/lib/app/app_lock_state_service.dart, which tracks last activity, backgrounding, and lock state with a 5-minute timeout.
2. [finished] WAH4PApp now listens for lock state changes and app lifecycle resume events, and routes to MPIN unlock only when the idle timeout has actually been exceeded and MPIN is enabled locally.
3. [finished] AuthSession still restores and refreshes sessions silently, but refresh failure no longer drives the app-level reauthentication routing path for normal expiry.
4. [finished] Login, MFA, password-registration, TOTP challenge, profile sign-out, security-settings sign-out, and MPIN unlock flows now reset or unlock the app lock service so the idle-lock state stays consistent with auth state.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/app/app_lock_state_service.dart — Replaced the background-only lock helper with a dedicated idle-lock coordinator that tracks activity and triggers the 5-minute lock. | Notes: Added timer-based idle tracking and lock state notifications.
2. [modified] frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart — Wired the app shell to the new lock service, activity tracking, lifecycle resume handling, and MPIN-lock routing. | Notes: Added root activity listeners and route-guard logic.
3. [modified] frontend/lib/app/app_startup_service.dart — Changed startup routing so authenticated users go to dashboard by default unless the app is actually locked for MPIN. | Notes: Removed startup reliance on ordinary token expiry for relogin decisions.
4. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart — Adjusted silent refresh failure handling so normal expiry no longer forces the app into a reauthentication flow. | Notes: Removed the reauth escalation from refresh failure.
5. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/mpin_unlock_screen.dart — Made MPIN unlock restore the idle-lock state on success and clear it on forced sign-out. | Notes: Replaced the old background-state reset behavior.
6. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/login_screen.dart — Reset or unlock the app lock service after successful login and on login-related session resets. | Notes: Keeps the idle timer aligned with the current auth state.
7. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/password_registration_screen.dart — Reset or unlock the app lock service when registration produces a new authenticated session or an auth reset. | Notes: Ensures registration flows do not inherit stale lock state.
8. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/totp_challenge_screen.dart — Unlocks the app lock service after MFA success so the new persistent session starts with a fresh idle timer. | Notes: Keeps MFA completion consistent with login flow.
9. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/presentation/screens/profile_screen.dart — Clears the idle-lock state when the user signs out from profile settings. | Notes: Prevents stale lock state after logout.
10. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/presentation/screens/security_settings_screen.dart — Clears the idle-lock state when security-setting actions sign the user out or force a login transition. | Notes: Aligns security actions with the new persistent-session architecture.

Summary: Adjusted the Health Records feature to be cache-first and preserve visible cached content during background refreshes. The shared data screen now accepts repository injection for easier testing, keeps cached records visible when the network returns an empty list, and still falls back to loading/error states only for true first-load/no-cache cases. The shared template no longer shows a loading overlay once entries are already visible, which keeps the UI smooth and consistent with the Personal Records behavior.

Facts:
1. [finished] Health Records now uses the same cache-first UX pattern as Personal Records: cached records render first, and loading UI is only shown when there are no visible entries yet.
2. [finished] HealthRecordDataScreen now accepts an optional HealthRecordsRepository so the cache-preservation behavior can be tested without reaching real storage or network code.
3. [finished] When HealthRecordsRepository.loadRecords returns an empty list after cached content was already loaded, the screen keeps the cached entries instead of replacing them with an empty state.
4. [finished] The shared HealthRecordScreenTemplate no longer overlays a loading indicator on top of already-visible entries; quiet loading state remains only for empty first-load cases.
5. [finished] A regression widget test was added to prove cached Health Records remain visible when the background refresh returns no new items.

Summary: Fixed the auth logout-on-expiry behavior by preserving the persisted session during refresh failure and treating it as a reauthentication/lock state instead of clearing storage immediately. The app can now continue to route to MPIN lock without discarding the current session data unless the user explicitly signs out or there is truly no stored session.

Facts:
1. [finished] AuthSession.refreshIfNeeded in frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart now preserves stored session data on refresh failure and sets the reauthentication flag instead of clearing the session for expired or invalid refresh results.
2. [finished] WAH4P app route redirection in frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart now bases the lock/login target on the reauthentication flag and MPIN availability while avoiding accidental route churn.
3. [finished] AppStartupService in frontend/lib/app/app_startup_service.dart still routes authenticated expired sessions to MPIN unlock when possible, so the app can land in a locked state rather than logging out immediately.
4. [finished] The MPIN unlock screen in frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/mpin_unlock_screen.dart still attempts to refresh the session before verifying MPIN, so unlock can restore access when the backend session is recoverable.

Summary: Adjusted the app to stop silently keeping users in the foreground forever and instead trigger a reauthentication flow when the session ages out or an authenticated API call returns 401/403. The app shell now listens for an explicit AuthSession reauth-required flag, routes to MPIN unlock when MPIN is enabled, and falls back to login when needed. AuthSession now exposes and clears this flag on successful login/storage restore, foreground expiry, and hard refresh failure. Several API clients now signal reauthentication on unauthorized responses, and manual sign-out paths clear the flag before wiping session state to avoid redirect loops.

Facts:
1. [finished] AuthSession now tracks a requiresReauthentication flag and exposes clearReauthenticationRequirement() so the app can distinguish session invalidation from a normal manual sign-out.
2. [finished] AuthSession._refreshSilently now requests reauthentication while the app is foregrounded instead of continuing silent refresh loops, which lets the app lock into MPIN after time passes.
3. [finished] Authenticated API clients for appointments, health records, medication resupply, interoperability, personal records, and auth security status now call AuthSession.requireReauthentication() on 401/403 responses.
4. [finished] WAH4PApp now routes to AppRoutes.mpinUnlock or AppRoutes.login when the reauth flag is set, and still handles resume/background unlock behavior.
5. [finished] Manual sign-out flows in login, MPIN unlock, profile, password registration, and security settings now clear the reauth flag before clearing the session to prevent unwanted redirect loops.

Summary: Implemented transaction-based correlation for sync receive-results while keeping the simulation path intact. The integration prepare flow now captures the gateway transaction id for each requested resource and conditionally stores a new fhir_sync_transactions row when the frontend provides the requester profile id via x-user-id. The FHIR sync service now resolves receive-results by transactionId first, falling back to identifier-based patient resolution when no transaction mapping exists, which fixes the Condition callback failure caused by missing patient inference. Frontend prepare requests now include x-user-id, and the app E2E plus backend integration/FHIR sync tests pass after the fix.

Facts:
1. [finished] The backend now persists fhir_sync_transactions rows keyed by gateway transaction_id when prepareSyncRequest is called with a requester profile id.
2. [finished] receiveResults now resolves the target profile by transactionId first before falling back to patient identifier matching.
3. [finished] The frontend interoperability client now sends x-user-id on sync prepare requests so the backend can store the transaction mapping.
4. [finished] Integration service prepareSyncRequest accepts an optional requesterProfileId so simulate can reuse the path without transaction persistence.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.repository.ts — Added sync transaction persistence and lookup helpers so inbound receive-results can resolve profiles by gateway transaction id. | Notes: Uses the new fhir_sync_transactions table.
2. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.service.ts — Changed receive-results to resolve profile ids from transaction mappings first and retain identifier fallback for compatibility. | Notes: Simulation can still call the same path without a stored transaction profile.
3. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.ts — Captured gateway transaction ids from prepare requests and conditionally stored transaction mappings for the current requester profile. | Notes: PrepareSyncRequest now accepts an optional requester profile id.
4. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.controller.ts — Passed x-user-id into the prepare flow so the backend can associate transactions with the authenticated account. | Notes: Returns a clear 400 if the caller lacks account context.
5. [modified] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/data/interoperability_api_client.dart — Added the x-user-id header to sync prepare calls so the backend can persist transaction correlation information. | Notes: Reads the user id from AuthSession.
6. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.module.ts — Exported the repository so the integration module can reuse it for transaction persistence. | Notes: Needed for the requester-side transaction mapping flow.
7. [modified] backend/src/supabase/database.types.ts — Added typed support for the new fhir_sync_transactions table in the generated database contract. | Notes: Includes row, insert, update, and relation metadata.
8. [created] sqls/012_fhir_sync_transactions_setup.sql — Added the database migration that creates the requester transaction mapping table and its indexes/policies. | Notes: Includes a profile-scoped select policy and updated_at trigger.
9. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.service.spec.ts — Added regression coverage for resolving receive-results by transactionId without a patient resource and updated existing mocks for the new repository contract. | Notes: Also preserves existing identifier-based behavior.
10. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.spec.ts — Updated the prepare-flow spec to mock multiple gateway request calls and verify transaction persistence behavior. | Notes: Simulation-friendly optional requester profile id is covered by the current constructor path.

metadata.summary: Implemented transaction-based correlation for sync receive-results while keeping the simulation path intact. The integration prepare flow now captures the gateway transaction id for each requested resource and conditionally stores a new fhir_sync_transactions row when the frontend provides the requester profile id via x-user-id. The FHIR sync service now resolves receive-results by transactionId first, falling back to identifier-based patient resolution when no transaction mapping exists, which fixes the Condition callback failure caused by missing patient inference. Frontend prepare requests now include x-user-id, and the app E2E plus backend integration/FHIR sync tests pass after the fix.

content:
Summary: Fixed a live gateway 502/400 receive-results rejection for Condition resources. The gateway sent a Condition in a Bundle with meta.profile set to the base FHIR profile http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Condition, while WAH4P backend required the PH Core suffix /phcore/StructureDefinition/ph-core-condition. The fix added a narrow Condition-only compatible profile allowance and improved Condition recordedAt mapping to use recordedDate/onsetDateTime/abatementDateTime/performedDateTime before period fallbacks. Parser/service/E2E tests passed.

Facts:
1. [finished] The gateway audit log failure was caused by backend Condition parser profile validation rejecting meta.profile http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Condition because it expected a PH Core condition profile suffix.
2. [finished] WAH4P backend now accepts base FHIR Condition profile http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Condition as a compatible profile only for Condition resources.
3. [finished] Condition health-record mapping now uses recordedDate first, then onsetDateTime, abatementDateTime, performedDateTime, onsetPeriod, performedPeriod, and finally current time for recordedAt.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.profile.ts — Added Condition-only compatible profile support for the generic base FHIR Condition profile used by the gateway payload. | Notes: PH Core suffix validation remains the primary path for all supported resources.
2. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.mapper.ts — Improved Condition recordedAt derivation so incoming Condition payloads with recordedDate or onsetDateTime map to the intended health-record date. | Notes: This also benefits PH Core Condition fixture mapping.
3. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/fhir-parser.registry.spec.ts — Added regression coverage proving generic base FHIR Condition profiles are accepted and mapped with the intended recordedAt date. | Notes: Existing missing-profile rejection test remains unchanged.

Summary: Implemented explicit transaction correlation for the FHIR sync receive-results flow. The backend now persists a fhir_sync_transactions record during processQuery after the target profile is resolved, then resolves receiveResults first by transactionId and only falls back to identifier-based profile resolution when no transaction mapping exists. This preserves PH Core validation while supporting Condition-only callbacks that do not include a Patient resource. Added schema/type support and regression tests; targeted backend tests and build passed.

Facts:
1. [finished] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.service.ts now upserts a sync transaction mapping during processQuery and resolves receiveResults using transactionId before identifier fallback.
2. [finished] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.repository.ts now includes upsertSyncTransaction and findProfileIdByTransactionId for the new correlation table.
3. [finished] backend/src/supabase/database.types.ts includes the new fhir_sync_transactions table shape used by the backend repository.
4. [finished] sqls/012_fhir_sync_transactions_setup.sql defines the new fhir_sync_transactions table, indexes, updated_at trigger, and deny-all RLS policy.
5. [finished] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.service.spec.ts now covers processQuery correlation storage, Condition-only receive-results using transaction mapping, and identifier fallback when no transaction mapping exists.
6. [finished] npm test -- --runInBand fhir-sync.service.spec.ts fhir-sync.profile.spec.ts and npm run build both passed after the correlation changes.

Affected files:

Summary: Fixed a real requester-backend contract mismatch found while debugging Condition receive-results failures. The backend receive-results handler previously only accepted a FHIR Bundle, but the live gateway audit log showed the gateway forwarding SUCCESS data as a raw array of resources for the Condition callback. I hardened FhirSyncService.extractBundle to accept either a Bundle or an array and normalize arrays into a collection Bundle before parsing. Regression tests now cover both the standard Bundle path and the array payload path, while the existing PH Core profile validation remains in place for Condition and other clinical resources.

Facts:
1. [finished] The live gateway audit log showed POST /api/v1/fhir/receive/Condition forwarding SUCCESS data as a raw array containing a Condition resource rather than a Bundle object.

3. [finished] FhirSyncService.extractBundle now normalizes array payloads into a collection Bundle so receive-results can parse either Bundle or array-shaped data.
4. [finished] Backend tests now cover both receive-results payload shapes: a Bundle payload and an array payload containing Patient plus Condition resources.

Summary: Resolved a Condition receive-results failure caused by PH Core profile validation being too strict about URL formatting. The backend PH Core profile matcher now normalizes profile URLs before checking suffixes, and the parser utility uses the same matcher. Added a regression spec proving the Condition fixture passes and that a harmless query/fragment/trailing-slash variant still matches. This aligns the requester backend with gateway-delivered Condition payloads that may preserve PH Core profile metadata but vary slightly in URL shape.

3. [finished] The new validator normalizes profile URLs by trimming, removing query/hash fragments, stripping trailing slashes, and lowercasing before suffix comparison.
4. [finished] A new backend spec file backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.profile.spec.ts covers the Condition PH Core fixture and a normalized URL edge case.

Summary: Adjusted frontend auth UX so expired but refreshable sessions route into the MPIN unlock flow instead of logging the user out. Session refresh now distinguishes between transient refresh failures and hard refresh-token failures, preserving locked state for the former and forcing login only for invalid/expired refresh tokens.

Facts:
1. [finished] AuthSession in frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart now tracks savedAt in memory, exposes isAccessTokenExpired, and schedules proactive refresh/retry behavior.
2. [finished] AuthSession.refreshIfNeeded now keeps a locked session on transient refresh errors but clears the session for hard auth failures such as invalid refresh tokens.
3. [finished] App startup in frontend/lib/app/app_startup_service.dart now routes expired authenticated sessions to AppRoutes.mpinUnlock when MPIN is enabled, instead of defaulting to login.
4. [finished] App resume handling in frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart now sends users to MPIN unlock when the session is expired, even if the normal background-lock timeout was not the trigger.
5. [finished] MpinUnlockScreen now refreshes the session before attempting MPIN verification so users with an expired access token can unlock without being forced through login first.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart — Centralized refresh/session expiry detection and preserved expired-but-refreshable sessions so the app can route them into the MPIN lock flow. | Notes: Added savedAt tracking, isAccessTokenExpired, and refresh-failure handling that differentiates transient and hard failures.
2. [modified] frontend/lib/app/app_startup_service.dart — Startup route selection now sends expired authenticated sessions to MPIN unlock instead of login when MPIN is available. | Notes: This is the main entrypoint change for the new lock-state UX.
3. [modified] frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart — App resume handling now checks for expired sessions and can push the MPIN unlock route directly. | Notes: This complements startup routing and keeps the UX consistent after backgrounding.
4. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/mpin_unlock_screen.dart — The unlock screen now refreshes the session before MPIN verification, so an expired access token can be renewed as part of the unlock flow. | Notes: This keeps MPIN unlock functional after access-token expiry.
5. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/data/auth_local_store.dart — The persisted session model already gained expiry helpers needed by the refresh/lock flow and continues to back the new logic. | Notes: Existing helper coverage remains in the auth local store test file.
6. [created] frontend/test/features/auth/data/auth_local_store_test.dart — Regression coverage for session expiry helpers remains relevant to the new lock-before-login behavior. | Notes: The test still validates expiry timing logic.

content:
Summary: Improved frontend auth-session UX so access tokens refresh proactively before expiry and sessions are not dropped immediately on transient refresh failures. Also refreshed session state on app resume before MPIN/security checks, reducing surprise logouts while keeping auth boundaries intact.

Facts:
1. [finished] Auth session state is stored in frontend/lib/features/auth/data/auth_local_store.dart and now exposes expiry helpers for absolute expiry checks and proactive refresh windows.
2. [finished] AuthSession in frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart now schedules refresh timers after login/restore and retries transient refresh failures instead of clearing the session immediately.
3. [finished] WAH4P app lifecycle handling in frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart now refreshes the session on resume before checking MPIN security state.
4. [finished] MPIN unlock in this codebase still requires a valid authenticated access token, so it is not an offline replacement for an expired auth session.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/data/auth_local_store.dart — Added explicit expiry helpers to the persisted session model so the auth layer can decide when to refresh before a token actually expires. | Notes: Added isExpiredAt and isExpiringSoon logic plus an expiresAt getter.
2. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/auth_session.dart — Centralized proactive session refresh, retry scheduling, and timer cleanup so token lifecycle is handled in one place. | Notes: Refresh is now scheduled after restore/login and transient failures are retried instead of hard-clearing immediately.
3. [modified] frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart — App resume flow now refreshes the session before resolving MPIN/security state, reducing surprise expirations after backgrounding. | Notes: This keeps resume behavior aligned with the new auth-session lifecycle.
4. [created] frontend/test/features/auth/data/auth_local_store_test.dart — Added coverage for the new expiry and proactive-refresh window helpers to protect the auth-session timing logic. | Notes: Tests cover absolute expiry and the 5-minute refresh buffer.

3. [finished] Flat PH Core PWD extension payloads using pwd-disability-type are now supported and map into patient_profile.pwdDisabilityType.

content:
Summary: Reviewed the name-field cleanup request and identified the exact frontend/backend boundaries. The app currently still exposes a separate second-name field in the registration and profile edit flows, while the backend profile DTOs and merge logic still accept and persist secondName. The received FHIR patient example with given=['Michael Christian','Aparicio'] should be interpreted in the UI as first name = Michael Christian and middle name = Aparicio, not as a second-name field. No SQL schema migration appears necessary because profiles already store given_names as a text array; the change is primarily field mapping and UI/API contract cleanup.

Facts:
1. [ongoing] The registration and personal information flows still render and submit a separate second-name field via shared name-form widgets and auth draft models.
2. [ongoing] Backend profile DTOs and merge logic still include secondName; current profile storage uses given_names text[] so no schema change is required for the requested rename/remap.
3. [ongoing] The FHIR patient example issue is a UI/contract mapping issue: given name element 0 should map to first name and element 1 should map to middle name in the profile edit UI.

Affected files:
1. [inspected] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/models/auth_api_models.dart — Defines the registration and profile draft contracts that currently include secondName and compose givenNames. | Notes: Needs a constructor and getter shape update.
2. [inspected] frontend/lib/features/auth/data/auth_api_client.dart — Serializes registration and profile update payloads, including the secondName field that must be removed. | Notes: Payload keys will need to match the simplified contract.

Summary: Implemented a backend fix so saving the Personal Information profile now mirrors PhilHealth and PhilSys identifiers into patient_identifiers. This resolves the receive-results patient matching failure caused by profile data living only in profiles.patient_profile. Targeted repository/service tests pass; a broader eslint check still reports formatting and typing issues in the touched files, so lint cleanup remains separate from the functional fix.

Facts:
1. [finished] Profile saves previously persisted philHealthId and philSysId only in profiles.patient_profile, not in patient_identifiers.
2. [finished] receive-results matches inbound bundles only through patient_identifiers system/value lookups, so missing rows there cause the 400 Bad Request match failure.
3. [finished] The new backend flow upserts canonical PhilHealth and PhilSys identifier rows into patient_identifiers whenever a profile is saved.
4. [finished] Targeted Jest tests for profile.service and profile.repository passed after the change.
5. [finished] A targeted eslint run still reported formatting and type warnings in the touched files after the functional fix.

Affected files:

Summary: Fixed the MPIN registration navigation crash by aligning the confirmation route type with the boolean result returned from the confirmation screen. Added a regression test that drives the MPIN setup flow through the app router and confirms it reaches the confirmation screen without a route cast failure.

Facts:
1. [finished] The MPIN setup flow in `frontend/lib/features/auth/presentation/screens/mpin_setup_screen.dart` pushes `AppRoutes.mpinConfirm` with `pushNamed<bool>`, so the router must provide a typed `Route<bool?>` for that path.
2. [finished] The registration crash was caused by `frontend/lib/app/app_router.dart` returning an untyped `PageRouteBuilder<dynamic>` for `AppRoutes.mpinConfirm`, which Flutter could not cast to `Route<bool?>` during `pushNamed`.
3. [finished] A regression test was added in `frontend/test/features/auth/presentation/screens/mpin_setup_screen_test.dart` to open MPIN setup through `buildAppRoute`, enter a 4-digit MPIN, tap Continue, and verify the confirmation screen appears.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/app/app_router.dart — Typed the MPIN confirmation route as `PageRouteBuilder<bool?>` so Navigator can cast the route result correctly during the registration flow. | Notes: This is the core fix for the crash.
2. [modified] frontend/test/features/auth/presentation/screens/mpin_setup_screen_test.dart — Added a regression test covering the setup-to-confirmation navigation path through the real app router. | Notes: The test fails if the route cast error returns.

Added a dedicated password history table, repository, and service to hash and compare recent passwords, wired it into registration and password reset flows, and updated database typings and module providers. Also added targeted unit tests for the new auth behavior and verified the backend build passes.

Facts:
1. [finished] Signup now rejects an email at the registration OTP request stage when that normalized email already exists in the profiles table.
2. [finished] Password reset now checks recent password history and rejects reuse before updating the Supabase auth password.
3. [finished] A new password_history_records table was added in sqls/011_password_history_setup.sql with RLS deny policy and updated_at trigger.
4. [finished] The backend auth module now provides PasswordHistoryRepository and PasswordHistoryService alongside the existing auth services.
5. [finished] Targeted backend tests for password history, registration email conflict, and password reset reuse prevention all passed, and npm run build in backend passed.

Summary: Implemented a reusable top-of-screen notification system in the Flutter frontend and migrated snackbar callsites to it. The app now mounts a global notification host from WAH4PApp, notifications slide down as neutral cards, and screens use AppNotificationCenter instead of ScaffoldMessenger snackbars. I also added a focused widget test for the new host and updated the sync-records screen test harness to include the notification host and dismiss the shared timer state.

1. [finished] A new global notification service lives in frontend/lib/app/app_notification_center.dart and exposes info/success/warning/error helpers with auto-dismiss timing.
2. [finished] The app root in frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart now mounts AppNotificationHost in MaterialApp.builder so notifications appear app-wide from the top of the screen.
3. [finished] The reusable notification UI lives in frontend/lib/core/widgets/feature/app_notification_host.dart and renders a neutral card with slide-down and fade transitions.
4. [finished] Snackbar usage was removed from the Flutter frontend callsites I updated, including appointments, interoperability, blood pressure, medicine intake, body mass index, temperature, and medicine add dialog flows.

Summary: Planned a repo-wide migration from ScaffoldMessenger snackbars to a reusable top-of-screen notification card in the Flutter frontend. Discovery showed about 57 snackbar callsites across 21 files, mostly in auth, PHR, profile, interoperability, and appointments screens plus one dialog. The proposed design is a shared notification banner component plus a global controller/service mounted at the app root so notifications can slide down from the top and work from any screen or dialog.

Facts:
1. [finished] The Flutter frontend currently uses many direct ScaffoldMessenger snackbars, with about 57 callsites across 21 files discovered in lib/features and one dialog widget.
2. [finished] The app root is WAH4PApp in lib/app/wah4p_app.dart, which already owns the MaterialApp and navigator key, making it the natural place to mount a global notification host.
3. [finished] The codebase already has shared UI patterns under lib/core/widgets/feature and lib/core/widgets/ui, so the notification card should live as a reusable shared widget instead of being duplicated per screen.

Affected files:
1. [inspected] frontend/lib/app/wah4p_app.dart — This is the MaterialApp entrypoint and likely host for the global notification system. | Notes: Contains the existing navigator key and theme setup.
2. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/constants/app_colors.dart — Needed to understand the current palette and avoid introducing conflicting notification colors. | Notes: Current primary color is purple, but the new notification should not rely on purple tint.
3. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/constants/app_border_radii.dart — Needed to match the existing rounded card language for the notification surface. | Notes: All shared radii resolve to a 20px rounded style.
4. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/constants/app_text_styles.dart — Needed to keep typography consistent inside the notification card. | Notes: Provides the reusable body/title styles used across shared UI.
5. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/widgets/feature/app_bottom_sheet_widget.dart — Shows existing shared surface composition patterns for reusable feature-level widgets. | Notes: Useful styling reference for a new notification card/host.
6. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/widgets/ui/buttons/primary_button_widget.dart — Shows shared widget organization and styling conventions for reusable UI primitives. | Notes: Confirms the repo favors explicit reusable UI components.
7. [inspected] frontend/lib/core/widgets/ui/inputs/bottom_sheet_select_form_field.dart — Shows how shared widgets are structured and how overlays are used in the codebase. | Notes: Useful reference for building a reusable notification host or overlay behavior.
8. [inspected] frontend/lib/features/calendar/presentation/widgets/calendar_view_mode_dropdown.dart — Contains an existing OverlayEntry pattern that can inform the new notification animation/placement approach. | Notes: Demonstrates the codebase already uses overlays for floating UI.
9. [inspected] frontend/lib/features/health_records/presentation/widgets/health_record_filter_dropdown.dart — Provides another overlay pattern and cleanup behavior example for floating UI components. | Notes: Useful for lifecycle and dismissal handling.

Summary: Implemented a first pass of UI-only fixes from the APC testing comments while explicitly avoiding sync/interoperability changes. Updated splash branding, added stronger password validation and a live password mismatch notice, improved the personal-information birth-date field with a date picker and MM/DD/YYYY display, and made the dashboard profile-completion prompt dismiss reliably. Added targeted tests for the new splash branding, password validator, and adjusted profile screen expectations. Verified the updated frontend tests passed.

Facts:
1. [finished] The work intentionally avoided any changes inside the interoperability/sync wizard area, per the user's request to not change syncing problems.
2. [finished] The splash screen now shows explicit app branding text ('WAH for Patients') alongside the animated slogan.
3. [finished] Password validation now requires a special character and the registration screen shows a live mismatch notice when password and confirmation differ.
4. [finished] The personal-information birth-date field now uses a date picker, displays MM/DD/YYYY, and serializes back to ISO format for save operations.
5. [finished] The dashboard profile-completion prompt now marks itself dismissed when the user closes or completes it, preventing repeated popup loops.
6. [finished] Targeted frontend tests passed after the UI changes, including splash branding, profile screen expectations, and auth validator checks.

Affected files:

Summary: Fixed the backend webhook routing mismatch that caused /api/v1/fhir/receive-results to 404. The global prefix in backend/src/main.ts now applies cleanly to all routes without excluding the FHIR webhook endpoints, the webhook controller remains mounted at @Controller('fhir'), and the e2e route test now boots with the same api/v1 global prefix used in production. Verification passed for both build and FHIR e2e routing.

Facts:
1. [finished] backend/src/main.ts now sets the global prefix to api/v1 without excluding the FHIR webhook routes, so the webhook controller inherits the same public base path as the rest of the API.
2. [finished] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.controller.ts stays at @Controller('fhir'), which combines with the global prefix to produce /api/v1/fhir/... routes.
3. [finished] backend/test/fhir-sync.e2e-spec.ts now sets app.setGlobalPrefix('api/v1') so the e2e test matches production routing and verifies /api/v1/fhir/process-query, /receive-results, and /receive-push.
4. [finished] npm run build and the FHIR e2e test suite both passed after the routing fix.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/main.ts — Removed the FHIR-specific prefix exclusions so the global api/v1 prefix applies to the webhook routes. | Notes: This is the production routing fix.
2. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.controller.ts — Kept the webhook controller mounted under fhir so it combines with the global prefix to form /api/v1/fhir/... | Notes: This file was previously corrected away from api/fhir.
3. [modified] backend/test/fhir-sync.e2e-spec.ts — Updated the e2e app bootstrap and expectations to match the production api/v1 prefix. | Notes: This ensures route coverage reflects the deployed behavior.

Summary: Relaxed personal information save behavior so the frontend and backend no longer require all fields for profile updates, while sync remains gated strictly by identifier presence. The backend now accepts partial profile drafts, merges them with existing profile data, and keeps the sync-lock enforcement introduced earlier. Frontend form fields were changed to optional validators and the sync button still depends on having at least one identifier. Regression tests were updated and targeted Flutter/Jest suites passed.

Facts:
1. [finished] Profile save/update now accepts partial drafts instead of requiring all personal information fields; missing values are preserved from existing profile data when present.
2. [finished] Sync readiness on the personal information screen is still identifier-gated only: the Sync records button remains disabled unless PhilHealth ID or PhilSys ID is present.
3. [finished] The profile form no longer marks fields as required in the UI and uses optional validators for all fields, matching the new save behavior.
4. [finished] Backend profile service now merges incoming partial profile drafts with existing profile data and still blocks edits when the profile has been sync-locked.
5. [finished] Frontend and backend regression tests were updated and the targeted Flutter and Jest test suites passed after the changes.

1. [modified] backend/src/auth/dto/update-profile.dto.ts — Converted the profile update contract to optional fields so saving changes no longer requires filling every personal information field. | Notes: All profile fields now validate only when non-empty.
2. [modified] backend/src/auth/profile.service.ts — Implemented partial-profile merge behavior and preserved sync-lock edit blocking. | Notes: Merges incoming drafts with existing row values instead of overwriting with empty strings.
3. [created] backend/src/auth/profile.service.spec.ts — Added regression coverage for partial save behavior and sync-locked update rejection. | Notes: New targeted unit tests for the profile service.
4. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/presentation/widgets/patient_profile_form_widget.dart — Removed required-field UI cues and switched the form to optional validation across all profile fields. | Notes: The widget now matches the relaxed save contract.
5. [modified] frontend/test/features/profile/presentation/screens/personal_information_screen_test.dart — Adjusted expectations so the sync button remains disabled without identifiers and the form no longer asserts required markers. | Notes: Also updated button style assertions to WidgetState.

Summary: Implemented sync/profile lock workflow across backend and frontend: sync prepare now triggers a real gateway Patient request, Patient resource mapping now hydrates internal profile fields and marks profile as sync-locked, profile API exposes lock state, and personal information UI now enables sync based on identifier-only readiness and disables editing when sync-locked. Updated regression tests for profile readiness and sync navigation behavior, and validated with targeted Flutter and backend Jest suites.

Facts:
1. [finished] Backend integration prepare flow now calls the gateway /fhir/request/Patient endpoint after provider/identifier validation, so Prepare request triggers an actual Patient data request instead of preview-only behavior.
2. [finished] FHIR Patient mapping now hydrates internal profile schema fields (birthDate, gender, phoneNumber, identifiers, address fields) and sets a syncLocked marker in patient_profile to indicate gateway-synced profile lock state.
3. [finished] Profile responses now include isSyncLocked, and profile update attempts are blocked when profile is sync-locked, preventing manual edits after sync hydration.
4. [finished] Frontend profile sync readiness logic now gates only on identifier presence (PhilHealth ID or PhilSys ID), not full profile completion.
5. [finished] Personal information form now supports read-only mode; when AuthSession.profile.isSyncLocked is true, the form shows a lock notice, disables interactions, and hides save/reset actions.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.ts — Prepare sync now performs gateway Patient request and refactored gateway request helper supports POST payloads. | Notes: Added requestPatientSyncFromGateway and requestGatewayJson method split for GET/POST.
2. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.spec.ts — Test expectations updated because prepare sync now performs two gateway calls (provider fetch + patient request). | Notes: Added second mocked fetch response for gateway request endpoint.
3. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.mapper.ts — Patient resource mapping now hydrates internal profile fields and marks profile as syncLocked. | Notes: Added identifier/address/telecom extraction helpers and lock marker assignment.
4. [modified] backend/src/auth/auth.types.ts — Profile API contract now includes isSyncLocked state for frontend read-only handling. | Notes: Extended PatientProfileResponse interface.
5. [modified] backend/src/auth/profile.repository.ts — Profile response generation now computes isSyncLocked and exposes helper for lock-state detection. | Notes: Added isPatientProfileSyncLocked utility.
6. [modified] backend/src/auth/profile.service.ts — Manual profile updates are now blocked when profile is sync-locked. | Notes: Added BadRequestException guard before upsert.
7. [modified] backend/src/auth/login-auth.service.ts — Default empty profile response now includes isSyncLocked for contract consistency. | Notes: Updated EMPTY_PATIENT_PROFILE_RESPONSE.
8. [modified] frontend/lib/features/auth/domain/models/auth_api_models.dart — Frontend profile model now reads/writes isSyncLocked from API responses. | Notes: Extended UserProfileSummary constructor, fromJson, empty, and toJson.
9. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/domain/profile_sync_readiness.dart — Sync readiness now depends only on identifier presence as requested. | Notes: Removed completeness requirement from readiness state.
10. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/presentation/screens/personal_information_screen.dart — Personal information screen now passes read-only state to form and uses identifier-centric sync guidance copy. | Notes: Uses AuthSession.profile.isSyncLocked for form locking.

The personal information screen no longer repeats a technical follow-up snackbar after the sync wizard closes. Added widget tests covering both dashboard-return success paths.

Summary: Fixed the sync simulation failure that surfaced as 'unable to store observation record' by making the backend observation/lab record filter text length-safe before insert. The root cause was an overlong laboratory_result_records.filter_value exceeding the database's 80-character check constraint. Added a reusable filter builder in the FHIR sync mapper so clinical record filter values are bounded, and added regression coverage for the PH Core parsers to assert filterValue length stays within 80 characters. Verified with targeted backend tests, backend build, and the sync simulation e2e route test.

Facts:
1. [finished] The sync simulation flow now authenticates with the Supabase access token and stores simulated results against the authenticated account context rather than a user-facing profileId.
2. [finished] The observation storage failure was caused by an overlong filter_value for laboratory_result_records, which violated the database check constraint of 80 characters.
3. [finished] A reusable length-safe filter builder was added to backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.mapper.ts and used for clinical record filter values.
4. [finished] A regression test was added in backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/fhir-parser.registry.spec.ts to assert clinical filterValue strings remain within 80 characters.
5. [finished] Verification completed successfully with npm test for the FHIR sync parser/service specs, npm run build, and npm run test:e2e -- --runInBand app.e2e-spec.ts in backend/.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.mapper.ts — Added the length-safe filter builder and updated clinical record mapping so inserted filter values cannot exceed the database constraint. | Notes: This is the primary production fix for the observation storage failure.
2. [modified] backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers/fhir-parser.registry.spec.ts — Added regression coverage asserting clinical record filter values stay within the 80-character database limit. | Notes: Covers the observation fixture path that previously failed.
3. [inspected] backend/package.json — Confirmed the available backend test and build scripts before running validation. | Notes: No file changes were needed.
4. [inspected] sqls/007_health_records_setup.sql — Confirmed the laboratory_result_records schema and its 80-character filter_value constraint to identify the root cause. | Notes: Read-only inspection only.
5. [inspected] backend/src/fhir-sync/fhir-sync.service.ts — Verified the persistence flow that inserts parsed clinical resources into the repository. | Notes: No code change required in this file.

Summary: Fixed the sync simulation auth-context problem by switching the frontend to send the authenticated Supabase access token as a Bearer Authorization header, and by making the backend simulation route resolve the current user from that token through AuthSupportService, with x-user-id only as a fallback. The request body no longer carries profileId, and the simulation still persists the PH Core bundle against the authenticated account via the existing receive-results-for-profile path. Validation passed after the change.

Facts:
1. [finished] The simulation flow now authenticates with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> from AuthSession.accessToken instead of a request-body profileId.
2. [finished] The backend simulation route resolves the current account through AuthSupportService.getAuthenticatedUserFromHeader when an Authorization header is present, with x-user-id retained only as a fallback.
3. [finished] The user-facing simulation request body now contains only provider and identifier fields; profileId is no longer exposed to the UI or request payload.
4. [finished] The task card 'Remove profileId from sync simulation request' was moved to done after the bearer-token fix and verification.

Affected files:

4. [modified] backend/src/integration/sync-simulation.service.ts — Resolved profile ID from the bearer token using AuthSupportService, with x-user-id fallback, before persisting the simulated PH Core bundle. | Notes: This is the core runtime fix for the missing account context error.
5. [modified] backend/src/integration/dto/simulate-sync-request.dto.ts — Removed profileId from the public simulation DTO so the body only contains request data relevant to the sync action. | Notes: Prevents users from having to provide internal UUIDs.

Summary: Adjusted the sync simulation flow so the user-facing request no longer carries a profileId in the body. The frontend now sends the authenticated account ID in an internal x-user-id header, while the backend simulation endpoint resolves the profile context from that header and persists the generated PH Core bundle through the existing receive-results-for-profile path. This preserves UUID-based internal storage without exposing profileId to the user and resolved the validation error the user was seeing.

Facts:
1. [finished] The simulation endpoint no longer accepts a user-facing profileId in the JSON body; it now reads the authenticated account context from the x-user-id header.
2. [finished] The Flutter interoperability client and sync wizard now send x-user-id from AuthSession.userId when calling the simulation endpoint, while keeping the body limited to provider and identifier fields.
3. [finished] The backend SyncSimulationService still builds a PH Core Bundle from checked-in example fixtures, but now persists it via receiveResultsForProfile using the header-derived account ID.
4. [finished] The task card 'Remove profileId from sync simulation request' was created and moved to done after the fix and verification.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/integration/dto/simulate-sync-request.dto.ts — Removed profileId from the public simulation request DTO so the request body no longer requires a UUID field from the user. | Notes: This is the main API-shape fix.

3. [modified] backend/src/integration/sync-simulation.service.ts — Updated the simulation service to require a header-derived user ID and persist the simulated bundle against that profile context. | Notes: No longer depends on a body-level profileId.
4. [modified] frontend/lib/features/interoperability/data/interoperability_api_client.dart — Changed the simulation client to send x-user-id as a header instead of profileId in the body, and added header merging support to the POST helper. | Notes: The body now matches the user-facing request shape.

The backend now exposes a simulated sync endpoint that reuses the existing prepare flow for validation, loads checked-in PH Core fixtures, and forwards a Bundle into the existing FHIR receive-results persistence path. Summary: Implemented a strict PH Core-aware FHIR inbound parsing layer for the backend webhook flow. The service now validates inbound receive-results and receive-push payloads through per-resource parser modules before mapping and persisting data, and the shared mapper was improved to handle array-shaped encounter code fields from PH Core examples. Added fixture-driven parser and service tests, and verified the backend e2e suite still passes.

Facts:
1. [finished] Added PH Core-aware parser modules under backend/src/fhir-sync/parsers for patient, immunization, encounter, observation, condition, procedure, and medication request payloads.
2. [finished] Parser validation now requires a matching PH Core profile suffix for supported resource types before payloads are accepted.
3. [finished] The FhirSyncService now routes receive-results and receive-push payloads through parseInboundResource before Supabase persistence.
4. [finished] The shared FHIR mapper was updated so encounter codeable-concept text can be read from array-shaped PH Core fields.
5. [finished] New parser/service specs use PH Core fixture JSON files from resources/examples/ph-core and the test suite passes.
6. [finished] Backend e2e tests still pass after the parser/service refactor.

Affected files:

Summary: Implemented the real outbound interoperability sync submission flow. The backend now sends sync requests to the gateway's /api/v1/fhir/request/{resourceType} endpoint using the configured requester provider ID, selected target provider, and patient identifier, then returns the submitted transaction details. The sync prepare endpoint now responds with HTTP 202 and the tests (unit, build, and e2e) all pass.

Facts:
1. [finished] backend/src/integration/integration.service.ts now performs a real POST to the gateway request endpoint for sync preparation, after validating the selected provider and identifier inputs.
2. [finished] The sync prepare flow now submits requesterId, targetId, patientIdentifiers, reason, and notes to the gateway and parses the returned pending transaction details.
3. [finished] backend/src/integration/integration.controller.ts now returns HTTP 202 Accepted for sync prepare submissions.
4. [finished] backend test/app.e2e-spec.ts and integration service specs were updated to expect the real gateway submission and transaction response.

Affected files:
1. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.ts — Implemented the outbound gateway request submission flow, response parsing, and reusable request helpers for sync preparation. | Notes: This is the core behavior change.
2. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.types.ts — Added request submission and transaction response types so the sync flow can return a typed gateway transaction. | Notes: Keeps the contract explicit and stable.
3. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.controller.ts — Changed sync prepare to return HTTP 202 Accepted, matching the semantics of a request submission. | Notes: Aligns the endpoint status with asynchronous processing.
4. [modified] backend/src/integration/integration.service.spec.ts — Updated unit tests to verify the gateway request POST and the returned transaction details. | Notes: Covers the new outbound request behavior.
5. [modified] backend/test/app.e2e-spec.ts — Updated e2e coverage for the 202 response and transaction payload on sync prepare. | Notes: Confirms the app-level contract matches the new flow.

Summary: Implemented the first-pass inbound WAH4PC/FHIR sync flow for wah4patients. The backend now has a dedicated fhir-sync module for provider-facing webhooks and practitioner discovery, routes are excluded from the global /api/v1 prefix where needed, patient identifier matching is backed by a new Supabase table, and inbound resources are normalized into the existing internal clinical tables instead of storing raw payloads. Validation and tests passed after the changes.

Facts:
1. [finished] The backend now includes a new fhir-sync module with provider webhook controllers, a gateway auth guard, a sync service, a sync repository, and mapper/types files under backend/src/fhir-sync.
2. [finished] A new Supabase bootstrap file sqls/010_patient_identifiers_setup.sql was added to store patient identifier mappings for matching inbound FHIR payloads to internal profiles.
3. [finished] The backend already had internal tables for immunization_records, medical_history_records, medical_consultation_records, and medication_resupply_history_records, and the new flow writes normalized records into those tables instead of persisting raw FHIR JSON.
4. [finished] Gateway-facing webhook routes are exposed as /api/fhir/practitioners, /fhir/process-query, /fhir/receive-results, and /fhir/receive-push, with the global api/v1 prefix excluded for those paths.
5. [finished] Environment validation now requires WAH4PC_GATEWAY_AUTH_KEY and includes WAH4PC_PRACTITIONER_LIST_ENDPOINT for the provider-side inbound flow.

Summary: Implemented clickable dashboard weekly metric cards and improved BMI startup behavior so the screen no longer flashes the add form before determining whether today's BMI already exists. DashboardMetricCard is now tappable via an optional callback, and DashboardScreen maps BMI, blood pressure, and temperature cards to their existing AppRoutes. BodyMassIndexScreen now sorts loaded history newest-first and renders a loading indicator while history loads, so the add tab immediately becomes the today's-summary state when a record for today is present.

1. [finished] DashboardMetricCard now accepts an optional onTap callback and is wrapped in Material + InkWell for card-level navigation feedback.

3. [finished] BodyMassIndexScreen now sorts fetched BMI history newest-first before storing it and shows a loading indicator while history loads, preventing the add form from flashing before the logged-today state is known.
4. [finished] The BMI screen still uses its existing today-logged summary card flow; no route names were changed and the existing route constants remain the source of navigation.
5. [finished] flutter analyze on the frontend package completed with only pre-existing warnings/info unrelated to these changes.

Summary: Replaced the dashboard Weekly Health Report graph with a simpler static summary card. The metric card now shows a compact latest-value view with a weekly tag and entry count, and switches to a short empty state when the week has no records. Removed graph-specific trend-point usage from the dashboard metric contract and weekly report calculation, and updated tests accordingly.

Facts:
1. [finished] DashboardMetricData now carries hasData and entryCount, and no longer carries graph trend points.
2. [finished] WeeklyHealthMetricSummary now tracks entryCount and hasData only; graph trend calculation was removed from the weekly report contract.
3. [finished] DashboardMetricCard now renders a static summary instead of a graph, with a small weekly tag, latest value, and entry count when data exists.
4. [finished] The empty Weekly Health Report state is intentionally short: it shows only a brief no-records message and a single instruction line.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/features/dashboard/domain/dashboard_models.dart — Dashboard metric contract now supports the static summary presentation via hasData and entryCount.
2. [modified] frontend/lib/features/dashboard/domain/weekly_health_report_calculator.dart — Weekly health summaries now expose entry counts and empty-state status without graph-oriented trend data.
3. [modified] frontend/lib/features/dashboard/presentation/screens/dashboard_screen.dart — Dashboard now maps the weekly report summaries into the simplified metric card data contract.
